data.body: The most well known work of systematic theology is probably the Summa Theologica by Thomas Aquinas. Aquinas was greatly influenced by Augustine, but unlike Augustine, he applied Greek philosophical approaches to his systematic theology. But that's another topic (cont)
data.author: did:plc:xb43bf5ocdhn7wxecocqgsqc

data.body:
And Luther was heavily influenced by Augustine. 

Now while Luther didn't immediately succeed, you DID see some of the reforms he espoused in the Council of Trent, and especially in the First and Second Vatican Councils. (cont)
data.author: did:plc:xb43bf5ocdhn7wxecocqgsqc
